projects.title: Freak-Cha
projects.description: A CAPTCHA-inspired challenge to distinguish humans from AI using facial expression recognition; awarded Funniest Hack at HackHarvard 2025. Built responsive frontend with Next.js, Tailwind CSS, and tRPC, integrating real-time facial recognition using MediaPipe FaceMesh and YOLOv8.

projects.title: VBook
projects.description: A JavaFX-based CLI contact manager optimised for developers. Led a team of 4 to develop and deliver a functional tool on schedule by overseeing design and implementation milestones.

projects.title: MiccDrop
projects.description: A humorous karaoke app that rewards off-key singing. Implemented real-time pitch detection and karaoke-style lyrics using the Spotify API.

projects.title: LinkedIn Shitpost Generator
projects.description: A satirical web app that generates absurd LinkedIn-style posts powered by AI. Enter a topic and get 6 different comedic personas — from tech-bro earnestness to Singapore uncle vibes. Built with Next.js, React, and Google Gemini via OpenRouter.

projects.title: Canvas Scraper
projects.description: A CLI tool that syncs Canvas course files to your laptop, filters junk (videos/textbooks), and emails daily summaries of announcements and assignments.

projects.title: NUS Aircon Checker
projects.description: A Telegram bot that checks your NUS aircon credits by reverse-engineering the EVS2 portal API. Type /balance and get your balance instantly.

experiences.title: Lead Developer, Project Aegis
points: Led full-stack development of a controlled AI-access platform for CTF competitions, enabling ~90 participants to query LLMs within admin-enforced rate limits and usage policies to preserve competition integrity, based off a RAG system, and secured by LLM-guard as middleware to prevent jailbreak.
Owned Docker containerisation, authoring multi-service Compose configurations for reproducible builds and consistent environments across local dev and production.
Built an automated E2E test suite consisting of 130 tests with Playwright, covering critical participant and admin workflows to gate deployments and prevent regressions ahead of the live event.
Reviewed and merged ~200 of 316 total PRs across a 4-person team, enforcing code quality, security standards, and architectural consistency as the primary gatekeeper for the codebase.
Drove product decisions as PM — scoped features around organiser requirements, prioritised the backlog, and coordinated a team of 3 contributors to ship on time for the CTF event.

experiences.title: Machine Learning Intern
points: Designed and implemented a modular machine-learning pipeline for data parsing, feature engineering, model training and explainability, enabling rapid experimentation.

Optimised models (Isolation Forest, One-Class SVM, Half Space Trees, Random Forest, Graph Convolution Networks and Large Language Model embeddings) to classify malicious SSL/TLS certificates, achieving a 0.994 F1-score.
Applied explainable AI (LIME) to surface influential features in anomaly classification, improving interpretability and stakeholder trust.

experiences.title: Strategic Digital Projects Intern
points: Curated technical content in collaboration with Microsoft and AWS to showcase emerging technology use cases in industryfacing workshops, resulting in 25% of attendees initiating GenAI projects with real-world impact.
Proposed and led a data consolidation initiative leveraging Salesforce to streamline cluster management and cross-team decision-making, reducing time spent locating data by 35%

experiences.title: Freelance Photographer
points: Managed end-to-end client relationships and vendor partnerships, increasing monthly revenue by 300% over 6 years.
Delivered high-quality digital photography and videography services that improved client retention and referrals
